name: Dot Expert — Legal
description: Free access to vetted legal experts covering incorporation (SUARL/SARL/SA), Startup Act labeling, contracts, IP, and regulatory compliance.
ideal_profile: Founders who are not yet incorporated or navigating Startup Act eligibility, IP protection, or first client contracts.
not_suited_for: Founders already fully incorporated with legal counsel in place and no open legal questions.
sequencing_note: Use before approaching any investor or signing any commercial contract. Legal structure is a prerequisite — do this first if the legal score is weak.

name: Dot Expert — Finance
description: Free sessions with finance and accounting experts covering financial modeling, fundraising strategy, grant applications, SICAR eligibility, unit economics, and investor-ready financial statements.
ideal_profile: Founders preparing for a fundraising round who need to clean up their financials, understand valuation basics, or navigate Tunisian grant and SICAR mechanisms.
not_suited_for: Very early ideation-stage founders who have no product and no numbers to model.
sequencing_note: Use after legal incorporation is sorted. A clean cap table and financial model are prerequisites for any serious investor conversation.

name: Dot Expert — GTM
description: Free advisory sessions with go-to-market and commercial strategy experts covering sales strategy, pricing, distribution channels, partnership development, and early customer acquisition.
ideal_profile: Founders who have a product and are trying to land their first customers or scale an existing customer base. Particularly useful for B2B founders.
not_suited_for: Pre-product founders — GTM advice is premature without something to sell.
sequencing_note: Use after achieving at least MVP stage. Most impactful when there is a working product and at least one hypothesis about who the customer is.

name: Dot Executives in Residence
description: A curated pool of 25+ senior executives across tech, fintech, commerce, industry, and manufacturing. Founders are matched for recurring advisory sessions over several months.
ideal_profile: Founders with a working product and a clear strategic challenge: scaling a team, entering a new market, preparing for a board, or navigating a pivot.
not_suited_for: Very early ideation founders without product-market clarity — the exec's time is wasted without something concrete to advise on.
sequencing_note: Use after the product exists and after foundational legal and financial hygiene is in place. Most impactful at seed or early growth.

name: Dot Camp
description: The Dot flagship seed hosting program. Selected startups receive workspace at The Dot Tunis hub, structured programming, workshops, and access to The Dot investor and partner network.
ideal_profile: Tunis-based or Tunis-relocatable seed-stage founders with a working product and at least early traction signals.
not_suited_for: Regional founders who cannot relocate to Tunis — Dot Camp+ exists for them. Also not suited for ideation-only founders.
sequencing_note: Apply after incorporation and after achieving at least MVP. Having initial customers significantly strengthens the application. Mutually exclusive with Dot Camp+.

name: Dot Camp+
description: A regional variant of Dot Camp for startups based outside of Tunis. Provides hybrid programming to reduce the geographic barrier between regional ecosystems and the capital.
ideal_profile: Founders based outside Tunis at pre-seed or seed stage with a working product or strong concept who cannot relocate to Tunis.
not_suited_for: Tunis-based founders — Dot Camp is the right fit for them.
sequencing_note: Mutually exclusive with Dot Camp. If based outside Tunis apply here. Timing is similar — after incorporation and with at least an MVP.

name: Dot Landing
description: A 4-month intensive support program exclusively for Tunisian diaspora entrepreneurs wanting to establish or expand their business in Tunisia. Covers legal setup, market entry, and local network building.
ideal_profile: Tunisian diaspora founders living or recently lived abroad who want to launch or expand in Tunisia.
not_suited_for: Non-diaspora founders. This program is exclusively for diaspora founders.
sequencing_note: Can run in parallel with Dot Expert Legal which is particularly useful for diaspora founders navigating incorporation for the first time in Tunisia.

name: Dot Services — Workspace
description: Hot desk and dedicated workspace access at The Dot Tunis hub. Includes reliable internet, meeting rooms, printing, and community access on flexible membership terms.
ideal_profile: Founders who need a professional work environment without committing to a full accelerator program.
not_suited_for: Founders whose primary bottleneck is not workspace but something more fundamental — no product, no legal structure, no team.
sequencing_note: Can be activated at any stage. Does not require program enrollment.

name: Dot Services — Events Space
description: Access to The Dot event venues for hosting workshops, investor pitches, product demos, community events, or corporate meetings.
ideal_profile: Founders or organizations that need a professional venue for external events — pitches, demo days, workshops, partner meetings.
not_suited_for: Founders who are not yet at the stage of hosting external events.
sequencing_note: Relevant at seed stage and beyond. Most impactful when actively engaging investors, clients, or community.

name: Dot Services — Studio
description: Free access to a podcast studio, professional AV equipment, and an Adobe Suite and Figma design workstation for content creation, brand building, and pitch material production.
ideal_profile: Founders who need high-quality brand assets, pitch decks, demo videos, or podcast content without the budget for a production agency.
not_suited_for: Founders whose primary bottleneck is not content production. Spending time in the studio while the product is unvalidated is a distraction.
sequencing_note: Use when preparing for a specific milestone — fundraising round, product launch, or partnership pitch — where professional materials will make a difference.

name: Meetup VC
description: Regular investor networking events at The Dot bringing together VCs, business angels, institutional investors, and selected startups. Pitch sessions, curated introductions, and panel discussions.
ideal_profile: Founders actively fundraising or within 3 months of opening a round, at minimum seed-stage with a product and early traction.
not_suited_for: Ideation-stage or pre-product founders not ready for investor conversations. Showing up without traction damages first impressions permanently.
sequencing_note: Attend after legal incorporation, financial model, and MVP are in place. Ideally after landing first customers. Do not attend as a first step.

name: DTC — AI Hub
description: Dedicated AI startup incubation within the Digital Transformation Center. Provides technical resources, AI-specific mentorship, and connections to public and private sector organizations seeking AI solutions.
ideal_profile: AI or ML startups at any stage from pre-seed onward building solutions for the Tunisian public or private sector.
not_suited_for: Non-AI startups — this program is sector-specific.
sequencing_note: Can run alongside Dot Camp or Dot Expert. Most impactful after the technical core of the product is defined.

name: DTC — I4.0 Hub
description: Industry 4.0 incubation focused on IoT, smart manufacturing, robotics, and industrial digitization. Provides technical infrastructure, sector-specific mentorship, and industrial partners.
ideal_profile: Startups working on Industry 4.0 solutions — IoT devices, factory automation, predictive maintenance, supply chain technology, industrial sensors.
not_suited_for: Consumer-facing or pure-software startups with no industrial application.
sequencing_note: Best entered at pre-seed or seed stage while the product is still being defined.

name: DTC — GSMA Lab
description: Part of the GSMA Mobile for Development network. Supports startups building mobile and connectivity solutions for financial inclusion, agriculture, health, and education.
ideal_profile: Mobile-first startups whose solution is tied to mobile connectivity, SMS/USSD infrastructure, or mobile money — targeting underserved populations.
not_suited_for: Startups that have a mobile app but whose core value is not mobile connectivity infrastructure.
sequencing_note: Relevant from pre-seed onward for mobile-infrastructure founders.

name: DTC — Soft Landing
description: Soft landing program for foreign companies wanting to establish a presence in Tunisia. Provides temporary workspace, legal and administrative guidance, and introductions to local partners.
ideal_profile: Foreign startups or companies wanting to test or establish the Tunisian market. Also relevant for diaspora founders incorporated abroad wanting a local legal presence.
not_suited_for: Tunisian-founded Tunisia-based startups — this program is specifically for inbound foreign entities.
sequencing_note: Use as a first step for any foreign entity entering Tunisia. Pair with Dot Expert Legal for incorporation guidance.
